text: Artificial intelligence is becoming more common in our daily lives, but it also raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias. If AI systems learn from historical data that contains unfair patterns, they may make biased decisions, for example in hiring or lending. To ensure fairness, developers must carefully check the data they use. Privacy is another key issue. AI often collects large amounts of personal information, so we need strong rules to protect people's data. Transparency is also crucial—people should understand how AI makes decisions. Ultimately, responsible AI development means balancing innovation with these ethical values to create technology that benefits everyone.
